problems.description: Companies rely on expensive external providers or agencies for repeatable data sourcing and enrichment workflows, paying recurring service fees for work that could be automated.
problems.affected_user: Growth, sales ops, or marketing teams at SMBs and mid-market companies
current_alternatives: Subscription data platforms with high per-seat or per-record pricing

problems.description: Organizations lack control over the targeting logic and output quality of their data pipelines because the logic lives inside a third-party service rather than in-house.
current_alternatives: Negotiating custom filters with data vendors
Building point integrations with no centralized logic layer

problems.description: Changing search criteria, scoring rules, or output formats in outsourced or black-box workflows requires vendor coordination and long feedback loops.
problems.affected_user: Operations teams and analysts who own the workflow
current_alternatives: Rebuilding ad-hoc spreadsheet processes each cycle

problems.description: Teams have no visibility into why specific records were included or excluded from a data set, making it impossible to audit, improve, or trust the output.
problems.affected_user: Analysts, compliance teams, and operators who consume the data

problems.description: Recurring, rules-based data workflows are executed manually or via expensive services despite being fully automatable, resulting in unnecessary labor or subscription cost for a predictable process.
problems.affected_user: Any organization running repeatable data collection or enrichment at regular intervals
current_alternatives: Recurring manual execution by an analyst
Expensive SaaS platforms that bundle automation with data access
